cards.grammarNote: Irregular verb. j'ai, tu as, il/elle a, nous avons, vous avez, ils/elles ont.
j'ai = I have (elided from je ai because avoir starts with a vowel). un travail = a job (masculine).
de l' is the partitive article before vowels for uncountable nouns: de l'expérience, de l'ambition.
avoir le temps = to have time. Tu as le temps ? is the informal version. Formal: Avez-vous le temps ?
avoir besoin de = to need (lit: to have need of). besoin de + noun/infinitive. d'aide = of help (elided).

cards.front: I feel like taking a break.
cards.back: J'ai envie de faire une pause.
cards.grammarNote: avoir envie de + infinitive = to feel like / to want to. J'ai envie de = I want to / I feel like.
avoir de la chance = to be lucky (lit: to have luck). de la is the partitive article for feminine nouns: la chance.
avoir l'air + adjective = to seem/look. Il a l'air stressé = he looks stressed. The adjective agrees with the subject.
avoir horreur de = to hate/can't stand. de + les = des. des réunions = (of) meetings.

cards.front: How many years of experience do you have?

cards.grammarNote: avoir + past participle forms the passé composé: j'ai fini = I finished. avoir is the auxiliary for most verbs.
